source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Core Financial Platform
source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, Stripe, and Robinhood. We’re backed by top investors such as Lux Capital, Forerunner Ventures, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.

For more information, visit Basic Capital’s website https://www.basiccapital.com.



You will

 - Join an elite team of engineers responsible for building systems that facilitate trading and money movement

 - Build and maintain core trading, financing, and fund management systems

 - Design and architect systems that orchestrate and reconciles complex flows of funds

 - Participate in architecture discussions, planning, and code reviews across the stack



Required Competencies

 - Strong experience with modern backend frameworks, API design, and event-driven systems

 - Strong architecture design and problem-solving skills

 - Proficiency in any of the following: Kotlin, Rust, Java, Go, C/C++, Python, Ruby, or TypeScript

 - Experience working with MySQL or PostgreSQL

 - Experience with modern build systems and cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, Azure)



Preferred Competencies

 - Experience building trading systems, lending systems, and asset management systems

 - Experience in fintech or early-stage technology companies

 - Experience working in Kotlin backends

 - Experience with Temporal for asynchronous workflow orchestration



Cultural Fit

 - Passionate about solving big social problems with pragmatic solutions

 - Aptitude for complexity and curiosity for financial markets

 - Enjoys learning new technologies and selecting the right tool for each job

 - Adaptable and open to contributing across the tech stack as the team evolves

 - Autonomous, high agency, accountable, and thrives in a fast-paced environment

 - Strong collaboration and communication skills, working effectively in cross-functional teams

 - Excited to be a part of an in-office team

source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Cultural Fit

source_jobs.title: Enterprise and Strategic Accounts Leader
source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, and Robinhood, and we’re backed by top investors such as Lux, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.



ABOUT THE ROLE

Basic Capital is building a new wealth system for everyday Americans, starting with the place most wealth is supposed to be built: the workplace.

We are reinventing the retirement plan experience for modern companies and their employees. That means building a product that is actually worthy of the teams shaping the future of the economy, and then taking it to market with force.

We are hiring an Enterprise & Strategic Accounts Leader to help us win the most important companies in the world. The kind of logos that define a category. The kind of deals that change the trajectory of a business.

If you want to sell a product that is consequential, controversial, and undeniably better, this is your arena.




REVOLUTIONIZE RETIREMENT FOR THE COMPANIES REVOLUTIONIZING THE WORLD

Join one of the fastest growing enterprise sales organizations.

Your primary responsibility is to close the likes of Figma, Anthropic, Stripe, and Coinbase, and tell them their teams deserve something better than Empower.

This is not a role for someone who wants to “hit quota and go home.”

This is a role for someone who wants to build an enterprise machine, win strategic accounts, and help define the modern retirement category.

You will deploy the full enterprise sales toolkit with significant freedom to grow Basic Capital’s enterprise business into the go-to choice of retirement plans for the world’s most innovative companies.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Close Strategic Enterprise Accounts

 - Own the full enterprise sales cycle from first touch to close

 - Win strategic logos that validate the category and accelerate growth

 - Run high-stakes deal processes with multiple stakeholders and complex buying committees

Build a Repeatable Enterprise Motion

 - Develop and refine messaging, positioning, and objection handling

 - Turn early wins into repeatable playbooks and scalable process

 - Partner with leadership to define pricing, packaging, and contract structures

Be the Voice of the Customer

 - Bring customer insights back into product, operations, and marketing

 - Identify patterns in why deals move and why they stall

 - Help shape the product roadmap by advocating for what enterprise buyers need

Operate Like an Owner

 - Work cross-functionally with product, legal, ops, and leadership to get deals done

 - Navigate complexity with speed, discipline, and creativity

 - Represent Basic Capital with confidence, clarity, and conviction




WHO YOU ARE

You are a closer with taste.

You can run a deal cycle end-to-end, but you also understand that winning enterprise is about trust, timing, narrative, and precision.

You have:

 - High horsepower and a strong bias toward action

 - Exceptional communication skills and executive presence

 - A track record of closing large, complex deals

 - Comfort operating with autonomy and significant freedom

 - A desire to win category-defining customers, not just incremental revenue

You want a role where you can build something real and be directly responsible for outcomes.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Requirements

 - Experience closing enterprise and strategic accounts in a complex sales environment

 - Strong discovery, qualification, and deal execution skills

 - Ability to navigate multi-stakeholder enterprise buying processes

 - Comfort with ambiguity and building in a

source_jobs.title: Head of Product Development
source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, and Robinhood, and we’re backed by top investors such as Lux, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.



ABOUT THE ROLE

Basic Capital is building a new wealth system for everyday Americans, and we are doing it by unlocking access to capital markets in a way the world has not seen before.

To do that, we need to rethink the entire retirement investing stack. Not just the UI. Not just the fees. The actual financial products themselves. The strategies, the wrappers, the incentives, the constraints, and the outcomes.

This is a role for someone who wants to redesign retirement investing from first principles and ship products that feel inevitable in hindsight.




BUILD THE FUTURE OF FINANCIAL SERVICES

As Head of Product Development, your primary responsibility is to fundamentally rethink, rebuild, and innovate across all aspects of retirement investing.

Your work will span both accumulation and de-accumulation, including launching new lifetime income products (annuities and retirement income solutions) and improving and expanding our suite of accumulation offerings across both registered and unregistered (exempted) products.

This is not a role for someone who wants to tweak existing investment products.

This is a role for someone who wants to create new ones, with creativity, rigor, and real-world consequences.

We need people to boldly design, scope, and deliver new branded products and strategies across the retirement investing landscape. Apply creativity and rigor to the design, structure, and launch of innovative investing products.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


OWN THE PRODUCT STRATEGY FOR RETIREMENT INVESTING

 - Define the product vision for how Basic Capital builds wealth over time

 - Design new investment products and strategies that align with our mission and customer needs

 - Translate market structure, regulation, and customer behavior into products that work in the real world


LAUNCH NEW DE-ACCUMULATION PRODUCTS

 - Lead the development of retirement income products, including annuities and lifetime income solutions

 - Build products that help people convert savings into durable, flexible retirement outcomes

 - Partner cross-functionally to bring these products from concept to launch


EXPAND AND IMPROVE ACCUMULATION OFFERINGS

 - Improve and scale our suite of accumulation products across registered and unregistered (exempted) offerings

 - Develop new strategies, packaging, and product structures that unlock better outcomes for customers

 - Iterate on product design to improve performance, simplicity, and adoption


BUILD BRANDED STRATEGIES AND MARKET-DEFINING PRODUCTS

 - Create products that are not only financially sound but also clearly positioned and branded

 - Design structures that can scale through distribution and capital markets

 - Build a product roadmap that compounds into a durable advantage for Basic Capital




WHO YOU ARE

You are equal parts investor, product architect, and operator.

You have strong opinions about how retirement investing should work, and you have the technical depth to build the products that make it real.

You have:

 - Deep knowledge of investment products and market structure

 - A builder mindset and an obsession with creating products that actually ship

 - High standards for rigor, clarity, and customer outcomes

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity with high ownership and high accountability

You want a role where you can invent products that change h

source_jobs.title: GTM Associate (GTMA)
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

As a founding GTM Associate, you’ll be on the front line of driving Basic Capital’s growth through high-velocity outbound and strategic outreach. The market for retirement benefits moves slow, so we move fast.

You’ll be equipped with best-in-class tooling and data to qualify and route all inbound opportunities generated by our demand generation pods.

Expect a steep learning curve, direct impact on the company’s growth, and the opportunity to move into an AE or leadership role within 90–120 days.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Source new sales opportunities through inbound qualification and outbound outreach

 - Contact 100+ prospective customers per day

 - Maintain accurate records and prioritize leads in our CRM

 - Work closely with growth to build & execute top of funnel strategy


WHO YOU ARE

 - Previously a top performer in any sales role (At least >1 year of sales experience).

 - Strong interest in leveraging modern sales, prospecting, and marketing technology (Clay, Unify, Smartlead, HeyReach, EmailBison, etc.)

 - High-agency self-starter driven to take initiative and create impact.

 - You’re always learning, push yourself to do great work, and hold yourself to a high standard in how you do it.


WHY YOU SHOULD JOIN

 - You’re joining at the earliest stage, where your ideas and actions will directly shape the trajectory of an industry disrupting company.

 - Work in a fast-paced environment where your contributions will have immediate, tangible effects on the company’s success.

 - We move fast and our people do too. We prefer to promote rather than hire, and will likely have many new roles coming in the near future.


ABOUT BASIC CAPITAL

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, WeWork, Block, and Robinhood, and we’re backed by top investors such as Lux Capital, Forerunner, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.

source_jobs.title: Senior Operations Associate (Onboardings)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BASIC CAPITAL

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, Stripe, and Robinhood. We’re backed by top investors such as Lux Capital, Forerunner Ventures, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in Tribeca, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.

For more information, visit Basic Capital’s website https://www.basiccapital.com/.




YOU WILL

 - Design and build the programmatic systems, automation, and AI-powered tooling that scale onboarding to many clients without scaling headcount

CULTURAL FIT

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Account Executives (AEs) at Basic Capital own deals end-to-end. This role involves navigating complicated multi-stakeholder processes in a full rip-and-replace sale.

While this role will begin by focusing on opportunities generated by our outbound team and those who come via inbound, we expect AEs to do a meaningful amount of hunting on their own.

Over time, the target size of employer that we expect AEs selling to should grow from 25-100 to 100-250 employee-count companies. As such, anyone in this role should feel comfortable navigating a quasi-enterprise sales cycle, involving multi-threading, stakeholder management, longer (60+ day) sales cycles, and greater complexity than SMB or startup SaaS.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the full sales cycle: source, qualify, and close new business opportunities

 - Consistently generate pipeline through outbound prospecting and inbound follow-up

 - Drive deals to close within defined ICP (25-99 employee segment, up to 100-250)

 - Be a student of pipeline hygiene

 - Partner closely with Growth to refine messaging, targeting, and top-of-funnel strategy

 - Contribute to building repeatable sales processes and GTM playbooks for future AEs to use


WHO YOU ARE

 - Proven top performer in a sales role (1+ years closing or quota-carrying experience preferred)

 - Highly motivated by targets, with a track record of meeting or exceeding quota

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, high-accountability environment

 - Strong interest in modern sales tooling and automation (e.g., Clay, Smartlead, HeyReach, Outreach, Nooks, Replit)

 - High-agency self-starter who takes ownership and drives outcomes

 - Committed to continuous improvement and operating at a high standard




WHY YOU SHOULD JOIN

 - Join at the earliest stage and directly shape the company’s revenue engine

 - High ownership, high visibility, and immediate impact on company growth

 - Fast-paced environment with clear paths for promotion and expanded responsibility

 - Strong preference to promote internally as the team scales


ABOUT BASIC CAPITAL

Basic Capital is a fintech company reimagining America’s $1T retirement system. We’re building the “mortgage for retirement”—unlocking market ownership and making wealth-building accessible to every American.

Our team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, WeWork, Block, and Robinhood, and we’re backed by top-tier investors including Lux Capital, Forerunner, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis.

Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re building a lean, high-performance team focused on tackling wealth inequality at scale.

source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BASIC CAPITAL

Basic Capital is building the Robinhood of 401(k)s — a modern retirement platform that gives everyday workers access to the full investment universe, including stocks, crypto, private equity, and venture capital, alongside a first-of-its-kind “Retirement Mortgage” product that enables leveraged investing.

We are operating in a $10T market that has seen little innovation in over 40 years. Since launching just over a year ago, we’ve achieved 30% month-over-month growth, scaled rapidly, and raised a $25M Series A at a $110M valuation. We are now approaching a $50M Series B at approximately $300M.

Our investors include Bill Ackman, Lux Capital, Forerunner Ventures (Kirsten Green), Henry Kravis, SV Angel, and Ron Conway — a group that reflects conviction in building a category-defining financial platform.

Our team includes talent from Goldman Sachs, Robinhood, Stripe, Uber, and top universities. Based in Tribeca, NYC, we are a high-intensity, high-performance team committed to reshaping retirement and expanding access to wealth.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Basic Capital is hiring a Chief of Staff to operate as the right hand to the CEO, Abdul Al-Asaad.

This is not a traditional strategic role. It is an execution-first position designed to serve as the operational backbone behind a founder operating at extreme velocity across fundraising, regulatory affairs, and institutional partnerships.

The CEO opens doors — with partners like Coinbase, Apollo, and government stakeholders. This role ensures those opportunities are captured, executed, and closed.

You will sit beside the CEO in every critical moment: meetings, inbox, calendar, and decision-making flow. Your mandate is simple: ensure nothing falls through the cracks.

This is a high-exposure role with direct access to top-tier investors, regulators, and industry leaders. Over time, for the right person, this role can evolve into a President-level position.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

Own CEO Execution Infrastructure

Attend all major meetings and capture detailed notes and action items
Drive all follow-ups, ensuring every opportunity progresses post-meeting
Manage CEO inbox to zero — triage, draft responses, and prioritize
Build and maintain daily execution plans and weekly planning cadence
Handle scheduling, logistics, and communications without prompting

Drive Critical Business Operations

Coordinate Series B fundraising, including diligence, documentation, and investor communication
Manage strategic partnership execution (Coinbase-level deals)
Support regulatory workflows across DOL, SEC, and government stakeholders
Build systems to ensure opportunities are consistently captured and converted

Enable Company-Level Outcomes

Support credit facility raises (hundreds of millions in scale)
Work cross-functionally with CTO, Growth, Operations, and Legal
Help establish repeatable execution infrastructure across the company
Ensure CEO time is focused only on highest-leverage activities


WHO YOU ARE

You are a high-functioning operator with strong executive instincts and low ego. You thrive in high-intensity environments and take pride in flawless execution.

You anticipate needs before they are stated. You are equally comfortable in elite rooms and executing immediately afterward.

You care deeply about follow-through, not just ideas.

You have:
A bias toward action and ownership in ambiguous environments
Exceptional organizational and execution ability
Comfort operating at high velocity (9am–9pm culture)
Strong judgment, discretion, and professionalism
Ability to engage credibly with senior stakeholders across finance, tech, and government


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Requirements

4–8 years of experience
Investment banking experience (Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, or equivalent; FIG, LevFin, or similar)
Post-banking operating experience (Chief of Staff, BizOps, Growth, or founding team)
Background in Financial Services or B2B environments
Strong executive function and organizat

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Infrastructure & DevOps
source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

You will

 - Be a founding member of the Infrastructure & Security team, responsible for the platform reliability, security posture, and compliance readiness that enables Basic Capital to scale with confidence

 - Own Basic Capital's ongoing SOC 2 Type II compliance, implementing security controls, documenting processes, and working with auditors to achieve and maintain certification

 - Design and implement comprehensive observability systems including logging, monitoring, alerting, and distributed tracing across our infrastructure

source_jobs.title: Lead Retirement Plan Specialist
source_jobs.description:
LEAD RETIREMENT PLAN SPECIALIST




ABOUT BASIC CAPITAL

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, Stripe, and Robinhood. We’re backed by top investors such as Lux Capital, Forerunner Ventures, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.

For more information, visit Basic Capital’s website https://www.basiccapital.com/.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Lead Retirement Plan Specialist to be a senior operator on our team, a trusted guide to our customers, and an operational ERISA expert. You'll work directly with plan sponsors on plan design, onboarding, and the compliance work that keeps their plans running clean.

This is a senior seat on a small, high-leverage team. You'll shape how plan consulting actually works at a modern recordkeeper.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Consult with customers on plan design: eligibility rules, vesting schedules, safe harbor elections, match formulas, profit sharing, cross-tested designs. Recommend what actually fits the client.

Onboard new plans: sit with customers through setup, translate their intent into plan document elections, and de-risk the handoff to ongoing administration.

Help with year-end compliance: ADP/ACP, 410(b), 415, top-heavy, coverage testing, 5500 filings, 1099-Rs. Know where every plan stands. Partner with our external TPA on execution.

Handle corrections: failed testing, late deferrals, missed matches, eligibility errors. Scope the issue, pick the right correction method, get it done cleanly.

Translate the regs: SECURE 2.0, LTPTE, hardship sourcing, QACA formulas. Turn regulatory changes into concrete plan-level decisions for sponsors.

Shape the product: partner with engineering on what should be automated. Explain the edge cases that break every off-the-shelf system. Your tribal knowledge becomes product.

Write clearly: emails that plan sponsors and auditors both understand.




WHO YOU ARE

5+ years of ERISA operational experience at a TPA, recordkeeper, or plan consulting firm. You've run year-end for a book of plans. You've handled corrections. You've explained a failed test to a founder.

Preferred: QKA, QPA, CPC, or ERPA. Some mix of credentials is a strong plus, but deep operational experience matters more than any single letter combination.

Technically deep on the operational side. You can talk through correction methods, coverage testing, and amendment timing without looking things up.

Customer-facing by instinct. You like sitting with plan sponsors and HR leads. You don't hide behind jargon.

Ambitious. You want to shape a function, not maintain one.

NYC-based or willing to relocate. This is an in-person role in Tribeca.

source_jobs.title: Senior Operations Associate (CS)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BASIC CAPITAL

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, Stripe, and Robinhood. We’re backed by top investors such as Lux Capital, Forerunner Ventures, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in Tribeca, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.

For more information, visit Basic Capital’s website https://www.basiccapital.com/.




YOU WILL

 - Own the ongoing client relationship after launch, serving as trusted advisor to administrators and champion for participants

 - Design and help build the programmatic systems, automation, and AI-powered tooling that scale customer success to many clients without scaling headcount

 - Run quarterly business reviews and drive engagement programs that lift participation and keep plans healthy

 - Deliver continued education to administrators and employees as new features and offerings roll out

 - Turn client feedback into prioritized signal for product, and work to iterate on the admin and participant experience

 - Assist the support team on escalations and help build the systems that let support scale



REQUIRED COMPETENCIES

 - Proven customer success or account management experience owning a book of clients, ideally in fintech, benefits, or retirement

 - A systems builder who has turned high-touch, manual client work into repeatable, scalable programs

 - A leverage mindset, using AI, automation, and tooling to scale impact rather than adding headcount

 - Working knowledge of 401(k) administration and what administrators and participants need, or the ability to ramp quickly

 - A track record of driving engagement, adoption, and retention across a book of clients

 - Comfort running executive quarterly business reviews and navigating multiple stakeholders



PREFERRED COMPETENCIES

 - Experience building operational tooling, automations, or AI-powered internal systems

 - Familiarity with retirement plan compliance and ERISA concepts

 - Background in employee benefits, HR technology, or financial wellness

 - Experience in fintech or early-stage technology companies



CULTURAL FIT

 - Passionate about solving big social problems with pragmatic solutions

 - Aptitude for complexity and curiosity for financial markets

 - Believes in building leverage, using AI and automation to scale impact as the company grows rather than defaulting to headcount

 - Enjoys learning new tools and selecting the right one for each job

 - Adaptable and open to contributing across the operation as the team evolves

 - Autonomous, high agency, accountable, and thrives in a fast-paced environment

 - Strong collaboration and communication skills, working effectively in cross-functional teams

 - Excited to be part of an in-office team, working five days per week from our Tribeca, NYC office

source_jobs.title: Head of Capital Markets
source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, and Robinhood, and we’re backed by top investors such as Lux, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.

For more information, visit Basic Capital’s website https://www.basiccapital.com.

About the Role

Basic Capital is building the future of retirement investing and the capital markets engine behind it has to be invented from scratch.

This is not a “run the process” role. This is a market creation role.

We’re looking for our in-house Lewis Ranieri — the architect of the modern mortgage-backed securities (MBS) market — to help us build the credit rails that will power a new kind of wealth system for everyday Americans.



You will

 - Build and maintain relationships with credit managers and institutional lenders

 - Develop deep relationships with:
   
   - asset managers
   
   - structured credit investors
   
   - banks / warehouse providers
   
   - capital partners

 - Lead fundraising initiatives alongside CEO including engaging investors

 - Monitor capital market performance and execute against insights observed

 - Identify market opportunities to scale credit offerings

 - Collaborate with teams to streamline capital market operations

 - Structure, negotiate, and execute credit facilities / securitizations / forward flows (as relevant)

 - Translate our product and underwriting into a story institutional capital can’t ignore

 - Work cross-functionally with product, finance, legal, and ops to scale origination safely and quickly

 - Create a repeatable machine: not one-off deals but a brand new market



Qualifications

 - Proven experience in capital markets, with a background in banking, hedge funds, or fintech including embedded lending

 - Demonstrated ability in fundraising and managing relationships with lenders

 - Excellence in communication skills with high-stakes relationships 

 - Excellence in financial modeling and skilled in using tools like PowerPoint and Excel

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, and Robinhood, and we’re backed by top investors such as Lux, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.



ABOUT THE ROLE

Basic Capital is building a new wealth system for everyday Americans. We are doing it by giving people access to capital markets in a radically new way, and we are doing it with taste.

In less than a year since launch, Basic Capital is already becoming a household brand. We have something interesting to say, and a truth to be (well) told.

We are hiring a Product Marketing Lead to help turn Basic Capital into a generational financial services brand. This is not “fintech marketing.” This is storytelling, culture, and distribution, executed with speed and precision.




BUILD A GENERATIONAL BRAND IN LEGACY SPACE

Join the ranks of one of the most well-regarded Marketing Departments in tech.

Your job is to relentlessly drive the Basic Capital brand forward as a leading innovator in fintech, and to push well beyond what is expected from a financial services company.

This is not a role for someone who wants to manage calendars and polish copy.

This is a role for someone who wants to build a brand people talk about, share, and trust with their financial future.

You will relentlessly drive the Basic Capital brand forward as a leading innovator in fintech and create a generational financial services brand. With unmatched creativity and freedom push well beyond what is expected from a financial services company.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Build a Brand People Actually Care About

 - Help shape and scale Basic Capital’s brand voice, tone, and creative identity

 - Turn complex financial concepts into simple, compelling narratives

 - Create marketing that feels inevitable, modern, and unmistakably Basic Capital

Ship Campaigns Across Channels

 - Launch and manage multi-channel campaigns across social, email, partnerships, and IRL

 - Build creative that breaks through feeds and conversations

 - Execute with speed, taste, and high standards

Tell the Truth (Better Than Anyone Else)

 - Translate product truth into stories that drive adoption

 - Create content that educates without being boring

 - Make the complicated feel clear, exciting, and trustworthy

Operate Like a Builder

 - Move fast and iterate. Ship, learn, improve, repeat

 - Work cross-functionally with product, design, sales, and leadership

 - Bring creative ideas to life with scrappiness and excellence




WHO YOU ARE

You are creative, sharp, and relentless.

You have taste. You know what good looks like. You can write, edit, ship, and iterate quickly. You care about craft, but you care about outcomes more.

You have:

 - A strong creative point of view and the ability to execute it

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity with high ownership

 - A bias toward action and shipping

 - The ability to balance brand and performance without losing the soul

You want a role where you can build something iconic, not incremental.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Requirements

 - Experience launching and managing marketing initiatives end-to-end

 - Strong writing and storytelling skills

 - Ability to run projects with speed, independence, and attention to detail

 - Strong creative instincts across content, campaigns, and distribution

 - Comfort working in a fast-moving environment

Bonus Points

 - Experience in fintech, consumer finance, investing, or benefits

 - Strong social instincts and an understanding of modern distribution

 - Experience

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We're hiring a GTM Engineer to own and continuously improve the technical infrastructure behind every campaign, sequence, enrichment workflow, and data pipeline that powers our revenue engine. This is a hands-on, high-agency role with end-to-end ownership. We expect you to raise the bar and move fast.

The GTM Engineer is the operational heartbeat of our outbound motion. You'll own the infrastructure, tooling, and workflows that enable our GTMA team to execute effectively at scale. This isn't an admin role, it's a strategic execution position focused on removing friction, optimizing sequences, and driving pipeline growth.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


OUTBOUND OPERATIONS & TOOLING

 - Manage and optimize our outbound infrastructure (SmartLead, Outreach, Nooks, HeyReach, and adjacent tools)

 - Design, build, and refine programmatic and personalized cold email sequences

 - Own list management, segmentation, and data quality for outbound campaigns, including enrichment workflows using Clay-like tools and data providers

 - Implement signal-based triggers to identify high-value accounts and coordinate across channels

 - Track, analyze, and report on outbound performance metrics (open rates, reply rates, conversion to meetings)


BDR/GTMA ENABLEMENT

 - Serve as the operational partner to the GTMA team: they execute, you enable

 - Implement feedback loops to continuously improve sequence performance

 - Troubleshoot issues with tools, sequences, and processes

 - Train new team members on tools and best practices


WORKFLOW & PROCESS OPTIMIZATION

 - Identify and eliminate operational friction in the outbound motion

 - Automate repetitive tasks and handoffs between teams, including via APIs, webhooks, and basic scripting (Python or JS)

 - Maintain documentation and standard operating procedures

 - Ensure data flows cleanly between systems (CRM, engagement tools, reporting)


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Outbound motion runs smoothly without the Growth Lead in the day-to-day

 - BDRs have the tools, sequences, and data they need to hit targets

 - Sequences are continuously optimized based on performance data

 - New campaigns launch quickly and cleanly

 - Pipeline generated from outbound is predictable and scalable




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2+ years in a GTM Engineer, RevOps, Sales Ops, or Marketing Ops role

 - Hands-on experience with outbound tools (email, calling, engagement platforms)

 - Comfortable with APIs, webhooks, and basic scripting (Python or JS) to connect and automate across tools

 - Comfortable building and optimizing cold outreach sequences

 - Ability to work autonomously and take ownership of problems

 - Analytical mindset, you use data to inform decisions and optimize

 - Clear communicator who can document processes and train others


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience at fast-growing B2B SaaS companies

 - Familiarity with intent data and programmatic prospecting

 - Experience with HubSpot or similar CRM systems

 - Track record of optimizing BDR/SDR motions

 - Familiarity with intent data and programmatic prospecting

 - Prior experience in sales operations or revenue operations

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer - Product
source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, Stripe, and Robinhood. We’re backed by top investors such as Lux Capital, Forerunner Ventures, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in Tribeca, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.

For more information, visit Basic Capital’s website https://www.basiccapital.com.



You will

 - Join a small, high-impact team responsible for delivering exceptional user experiences across iOS, Android, and web platforms

 - Design and implement our new developer-facing API that will power third-party integrations

 - Architect and ship AI-powered features that enhance the user experience

 - Own the full product development lifecycle from design collaboration through deployment and monitoring

 - Participate in architecture discussions, planning, and code reviews across the stack



Required Competencies

 - Experience designing foundational architectures for mobile and web applications

 - Product mindset and deep experience working with product managers and designers

 - Proficiency in any of the following: Kotlin, Swift, React, React Native, or JavaScript/TypeScript



Preferred Competencies

 - Experience shipping React Native applications

 - Experience designing and documenting developer APIs

 - Experience in fintech or early-stage technology companies



Cultural Fit

 - Passionate about solving big social problems with pragmatic solutions

 - Aptitude for complexity and curiosity for financial markets

 - Enjoys learning new technologies and selecting the right tool for each job

 - Adaptable and open to contributing across the tech stack as the team evolves

 - Autonomous, high agency, accountable, and thrives in a fast-paced environment

 - Strong collaboration and communication skills, working effectively in cross-functional teams

 - Excited to be a part of an in-office team

source_jobs.title: GM, Consumer
source_jobs.description:
About Basic Capital

Basic Capital is a fintech company disrupting America’s $1T retirement industry by building the mortgage for retirement, unlocking market ownership, and making wealth accessible to every American. Our mission is to create products, platforms, and a credit marketplace that power a fundamentally new retirement system.

Our founding team includes leaders from Goldman Sachs, Uber, Block, and Robinhood, and we’re backed by top investors such as Lux, BoxGroup, SVAngel, Inspired Capital, and Henry Kravis. Based in SoHo, NYC, we’re assembling a lean, high-performance team committed to reducing wealth inequality.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Basic Capital is building a new wealth system for everyday Americans — and we’re doing it by unlocking access to capital markets in a way the world hasn’t seen before.

So far, we’ve built serious momentum in our B2B Employer business. But something else has been happening in parallel: our consumer product is pulling investors toward us — people who want more participation in financial markets, and who believe the old retirement system is broken.

Now is the time to go on offense.




TRULY OWN THE GROWTH OF CATEGORY DEFINING PRODUCT

Your job is to build the consumer product and business: product strategy, customer experience, growth loops, positioning, and execution.

This is not a role for someone who wants to “manage a roadmap.”

This is a role for someone who wants to lead a campaign and take an ambitious financial product from “people are curious” to “this is inevitable.”

Be directly responsible for the proliferation of one of the most innovative, unique, consequential, and controversial financial products: The Retirement Mortgage.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Own the Consumer Business

 - Build and run the end-to-end consumer product strategy

 - Define the consumer operating model: what we build, who we serve, and how we win

 - Be accountable for consumer outcomes (adoption, engagement, retention, unit economics)

Create the Consumer Brand

 - Make Basic Capital the go-to consumer brand for modern retirement investing

 - Translate a complex product into a story people instantly understand and want

 - Set the tone for consumer UX: simple, powerful, trusted, inevitable

Build Growth Loops

 - Develop distribution and growth strategies that fit the product (not gimmicks)

 - Design onboarding, activation, and education that convert intent into action

 - Create systems that scale: lifecycle, engagement, referrals, conversion




WHO YOU ARE

You are a builder with taste and a strategist who can execute.

You have:

 - Strong product instincts and deep empathy for consumers

 - The ability to simplify the complex without dumbing it down

 - A bias toward action: you ship, iterate, and compound learning

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity with high ownership and high accountability

You want a role where you can truly own the business, not just influence it.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Requirements

 - Experience owning and shipping consumer products end-to-end

 - Strong product strategy + execution ability (discovery → delivery → iteration)

 - Excellent cross-functional leadership (design, engineering, operations, marketing)

 - Ability to operate with high agency in a fast-moving environment

Bonus Points

 - Fintech / investing / consumer finance experience

 - Experience building consumer brands or consumer growth loops

 - Familiarity with retirement, brokerage, credit, lending, or structured financial products

 - You’ve built products people love — especially ones that were initially “hard to explain”

notes: Fintech company with strong engineering focus, backed by top investors.
